messages.text: Rig automation still assumes clean restarts, which is optimistic bordering on fictional for this phase.
Please do not call a deployment stable if it only survived one replay loop and no warm restart.
Quick platform note on telemetry backlog: event volume is outpacing the dashboard assumptions again, so if your graph looks clean please sanity-check the raw event stream before trusting it.
Cross-service metadata drift is making the dashboards less useful than the raw logs again.
Cache flush should be explicit in the API contract, not an unwritten step buried in a helper script.
Please include whether the rig was warm or cold before rollout. Half the debugging thread disappears if that detail is missing.
Rig automation still assumes clean restarts, which is optimistic bordering on fictional for this phase.
Please do not call a deployment stable if it only survived one replay loop and no warm restart.
If the fix depends on everyone remembering an extra script step, it is not really fixed.
Cross-service metadata drift is making the dashboards less useful than the raw logs again.
Cache flush should be explicit in the API contract, not an unwritten step buried in a helper script.
Diagnostics sampler overhead is back in the conversation. The rig tolerates it until somebody enables the louder profile and then timing starts to wobble.

Cross-service metadata drift is making the dashboards less useful than the raw logs again.

I trimmed one payload shape, but the bigger issue is still that replay and deployment metadata are drifting apart just enough to break simple correlations.
I can live with instability. I cannot live with a deployment note that says 'seems fine so far'.
Rig automation still assumes clean restarts, which is optimistic bordering on fictional for this phase.
Please do not call a deployment stable if it only survived one replay loop and no warm restart.
Calibration cache behavior still feels under-specified. The checksum can look perfect while the runtime state is clearly older than it should be.

I am okay paying a little overhead for evidence, but not enough to create the symptom we are trying to explain.
